senses.definition: to try one's best in order to solve a problem or to understand it

examples.x: He's been beating his brains out trying to finish that project on time.
She beat her brains out studying for the exam, but it was just too difficult.
I've been beating my brains out trying to figure out this puzzle, but I can't solve it.

senses.definition: to confuse someone by using difficult or technical words while describing something to them

examples.x: When I asked him to explain how the computer works, he started using so much jargon that he completely blinded me with science.
The manual for this software is written in such a way that it blinds users with science instead of providing clear instructions.
I don't need you to blind me with science; just tell me how to fix the problem in simple terms.

senses.definition: used to describe a situation in which someone remains uninformed or unaware, typically after an event or the receipt of information

examples.x: After reading the confusing manual, I'm still none the wiser about how to assemble the furniture.
He tried to explain the complex scientific theory to me, but I walked away none the wiser.
Despite attending the meeting, I left none the wiser about the company's future plans.
I watched the entire documentary on the topic, but I'm none the wiser about its significance.

senses.definition: to fail to correctly understand or interpret what someone said

examples.x: I'm sorry for bringing you the wrong order, sir, I've had my wires crossed all day!
I think we got our wires crossed because I was expecting you at 3 PM, not 3 AM.
We had our wires crossed when discussing the project, and now we're working on completely different tasks.
It appears we had our wires crossed about the meeting location, as I waited at the wrong café.

senses.definition: to be unable to comprehend what is happening around one

examples.x: During the movie, I completely lost the plot and couldn't follow the storyline.
I was giving a presentation, but I lost the plot halfway through and had to ask for help.
He was explaining the math problem to me, but I lost the plot after the second step.
She's been acting so strangely lately, like she's lost the plot or something.

senses.definition: a state in which one does not understand the current situation due to being completely detached from reality

examples.x: If Tom thinks he'll be able to live off his bad poetry, he's living in la-la land!
You're living in La-La land if you think you can quit your job and become a famous actor overnight.
His head is always in the clouds, living in la-la land, and not dealing with real-life responsibilities.

senses.definition: used when one is completely unable to guess or understand something

examples.x: Beats me why he wants such a big car.
It beats me how she got the job.
What beats me is why she stays with him.
Sorry, it beats me.
Beats me.

senses.definition: used as a response to mean that one does not have any answer to a particular question

examples.x: Search me.
Search me.
Search me.
Sorry, search me.

senses.definition: in a state or place that is unknown or unseen, particularly referring to where people go after they die

examples.x: The ancient texts provide insights into the mysteries of the universe, offering a glimpse beyond the veil of human knowledge.
The artist's paintings seem to capture moments from beyond the veil of time, evoking a sense of nostalgia and wonder.
After the near-death experience, she felt like she had briefly glimpsed beyond the veil of life and death.
The novel delves into the supernatural, taking readers on a journey beyond the veil of reality into a world of magic and mystery.

senses.definition: used to refer to something that is complicated or difficult beyond one's understanding or capability

examples.x: The legal documents were so complex that they were over her head, and she needed a lawyer to explain them.
His jokes are always over my head; I just don't get his sense of humor.

senses.definition: a subject that is very challenging or boring to one

examples.x: Quantum physics is a blind spot for me; I just can't wrap my head around it.
Her blind spot is history; she's never been interested in it.
I have a blind spot for financial matters; I struggle with budgeting and investments.
Statistical analysis is definitely a blind spot in my skillset.

word_list.pronunciation: biː ɔːɹ sˈaʊnd ˈɔːl ɔːɹ lˈaɪk ɡɹˈiːk tʊ ˌɛsbˈiː

senses.definition: used for saying that one is unable to understand the meaning of something particular

examples.x: He seemed to talk sense but it sounded all Greek to me.
These technical specifications are like Greek to him, as he has no background in engineering.
